questions.question: What is the default value of justify-content?
questions.explanation: The default value of justify-content is flex-start, which packs items at the start of the main axis.

questions.question: Which justify-content value centers items on the main axis?
questions.explanation: center centers flex items along the main axis.

questions.question: Which value puts the first and last items at the container edges?
questions.explanation: space-between puts the first item at the start and the last at the end, with equal space between.

questions.question: What is the difference between space-around and space-evenly?
options: space-around has half-size edges; space-evenly has equal spacing
space-around is the same as center
questions.explanation: space-around gives each item half-size margins on edges; space-evenly makes all spacing identical.

questions.question: With flex-direction: column, how does justify-content align items?
questions.explanation: With column, the main axis is vertical, so justify-content controls vertical alignment.

questions.question: Which justify-content value would you use for a centered navigation bar?
questions.explanation: center centers navigation items along the main axis.

questions.question: Which value distributes free space equally before, between, and after items?
questions.explanation: space-evenly distributes equal space before, between, and after every item.

questions.question: What does justify-content: flex-end do?
options: Aligns items to the end of the main axis
questions.explanation: flex-end packs items at the end of the main axis.

questions.question: Which axis does justify-content align along?
questions.explanation: justify-content aligns items along the main axis.

questions.question: What happens to justify-content when items exceed the container width?
options: Overflow behavior depends on the value
questions.explanation: With overflow, flex-start causes overflow at the end, flex-end at the start, center overflows both sides.

questions.question: Which value is best for a header with logo on left and nav on right?
questions.explanation: space-between pushes the logo to the left and nav items to the right.

questions.question: Can justify-content be used with flex-wrap: wrap?
questions.explanation: With wrapping, justify-content applies to each line independently.

questions.question: What does 'justify-content: safe center' do?
options: Centers only if items don't overflow, else aligns at start
questions.explanation: safe keyword prevents clipping by changing alignment to start if overflow would occur.

questions.question: Which justify-content value creates equal space BETWEEN items only?
questions.explanation: space-between only adds space between items, not at the edges.

questions.question: How does justify-content distribute space?
options: By distributing free space in the container
questions.explanation: justify-content distributes the free space remaining after items are laid out.

questions.question: What is the effect of justify-content when all items have flex: 1?
options: No effect, items fill the space
questions.explanation: With flex: 1 (grow), there's no free space to distribute, so justify-content has no visible effect.

questions.question: Which property is the cross-axis equivalent of justify-content?
questions.explanation: align-items aligns items on the cross axis; align-content distributes lines.

questions.question: With flex-direction: row-reverse, where does justify-content: flex-start place items?
questions.explanation: With row-reverse, the main axis start is on the right, so flex-start aligns to the right.

questions.question: What is the main difference between space-around and space-evenly?
options: space-around has half-size spacing at edges
questions.explanation: space-around gives half-size space at edges; space-evenly gives equal space everywhere.

questions.question: Which value would you use for evenly spaced buttons in a toolbar?
questions.explanation: space-evenly creates identical spacing between all buttons and the edges.
